calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial/Evidentiary Hearing Re: Will Contest
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for petition and proposed administrator Joseph Delanor Clemons, who was also present (via Zoom).
Wayne A. Lampert for petitioner and proposed executor Karen Louise Dexter, who was also present (via Zoom).
Kimberly Clemons, heir and interested party, was present via Zoom.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Petition for Letters of Administration; 2) Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary; and 3) Evidentiary Hearing/Will Contest follow up.

The Court stated that following the evidentiary hearing that occurred August 29, 2025, it had reached a conclusion and determined that the document dated September 30, 2023, constituted a holographic will. The Court stated its intention to admit the holographic will to probate and to issue a written order on the will contest at a later date.

No objection was raised to proceeding in that manner. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The holographic will dated September 20, 2023, is admitted to probate. Karen Louise Dexter is appointed as the Executor with full authority. Bond is set at $855,000. Order for the same signed in open Court. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for April 19, 2027, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. If either petition is filed two weeks prior, no appearance is required on that date. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee.

Mr. Lampert requested that the Court consider reducing the bond to the statutory minimum of $20,000, noting that the appointed executor is the mother of the primary beneficiary, Kimberly Clemons, that they reside together and maintain a close relationship, and that the estate presently lacks liquid assets. Mr. Robinson objected to the request and argued that the will's terms could be interpreted as providing a leasehold or life estate and maintained that a full bond was necessary to protect the estate's assets. After consideration, the Court declined to reduce the bond and reaffirmed the amount at $855,000.

The Petition for Letters of Administration was denied in light of the ruling on Petition for Probate of Will.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2025 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Oct-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Oct-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial/Evidentiary Hearing Re: Will Contest
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Continued To Oct-20-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Sep-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for Joseph Delanor Clemons, Petitioner/Objector/Proposed Administrator (via Zoom).
Wayne A. Lampert for Karen Louise Dexter, Petitioner/Objector/Proposed Executor (via Zoom).

The Court confirmed that all papers, including recent filings by Mr. Lampert, had been reviewed. Mr. Robinson confirmed service of the will contest on the decedent's sons and provided an offer of proof. He intends to call Joseph Clemens and a former attorney to testify regarding the decedent's expressed intent to create a living trust, arguing that the contested document was merely a checklist, not a valid holographic will.

Mr. Lampert responded that the contested document was written in the decedent's handwriting, signed and dated, and included detailed instructions for post-death asset distribution-meeting the requirements of Probate Code Sec. 6111 for a holographic will. He argued that evidence offered by Mr. Robinson was already contained in written filings and declarations and did not establish clear and convincing evidence to rebut the presumption of validity.

The Court discussed the legal threshold under Probate Code Sec. 6111 and noted it is obligated to allow the will contest to proceed. Both parties provided time estimates of approximately two hours each for their case-in-chief. The Court determined the matter could be accommodated in Department 204.

The Court set the Will Contest for trial/evidentiary hearing on August 29, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 204. By stipulation of counsel, all parties, witnesses and counsel may appear via Zoom. All underlying petitions were continued to September 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Sep-08-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ray Robinson for and with Joseph Delanor Clemons, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Wayne Lampert for Karen Dexter, Objector (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding the original holographic will, that it is in the possession of Mr. Lampert and he will lodge it with the Courts. Both petitions are continued to June 16, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ray Robinson for Joseph Delanor Clemons, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Wayne Lampert for Karen Dexter, Objector (via Zoom).

Mr. Lampert, newly retained by Karen Dexter, stated that his client intends to file a petition for probate to admit a holographic will into probate. The holographic will is already included in Mr. Robinson's petition. The court inquired about the timeline for filing the competing petition, and Mr. Lampert requested six weeks to complete the filing, including publication requirements. Mr. Robinson agreed to a six-week continuance to allow time for the competing petition to be filed and heard concurrently.

The court determined that both petitions should be heard on the same day for judicial efficiency. The court continued the petition for letters of administration to April 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Lampert was directed by the court to file his competing petition for probate of the holographic will with April 28, 2025, as the requested hearing date. It was suggested that Mr. Lampert include a note in the e-filing notes stating that "Judge Moody authorized the April 28 hearing date in open court on March 12, 2025."

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

payments.description: PETN FOR LETTR OF ADMIN, TESTAMENTARY, WILL CONTST
